refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined productivity target by optimizing equipment operation, task scheduling, and resource utilization.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level. Refining it into sub-FRs focused on key productivity drivers (equipment operation efficiency, task scheduling, resource utilization) allows targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations and verify that each sub-FR contributes to meeting or exceeding the target.
sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operation to maximize throughput.
FR2: Schedule tasks efficiently to minimize idle time.
FR3: Utilize resources effectively to support continuous operation.

candidate_dps: DP1: Advanced vehicle control algorithms for operation optimization.
DP3: Resource monitoring and allocation framework.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Advanced vehicle control algorithms for operation optimization.

potential_sources: Interaction between vehicle control and task scheduling
coupling_check.notes: DP1 is logically separable but requires interface coordination with task scheduling and resource management to avoid tight coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes vehicle control algorithms can be modularized independently.
Risk that poor interface design may increase coupling and reduce modularity.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicit interface definitions with task scheduling and resource modules to reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and software components responsible for vehicle operation and control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle control module outputs operational data for monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task commands and schedules from the task scheduling system.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication for remote control and data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope, less focused containment for vehicle control DP.
High containment score as vehicle control DP logically fits here with minimal diffusion.
Teleoperation involves control but focuses on remote operation, less suitable for core vehicle control DP.
Safety services are peripheral to vehicle control DP.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the vehicle control DP to the Vehicle module maximizes containment and minimizes diffusion, supporting clear ownership and modularity.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules.
Risk of overlapping responsibilities if task scheduling logic is embedded in Vehicle module.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and boundaries with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules to mitigate diffusion risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are logically decomposed and the selected DP addresses a key sub-FR with clear mapping.

diffusion_risks: Interfaces with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules may cause diffusion if not well managed.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment for the selected DP, supporting modular ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring vehicle operation metrics and telemetry data.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of vehicle control DP to Vehicle module supports traceability and ownership.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface definitions with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules.
Consider revising task scheduling DP assignment in next decomposition level.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, interface definitions and coupling assumptions require refinement to reduce diffusion risks before proceeding.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operation to maximize throughput.
FR2: Schedule tasks efficiently to minimize idle time.
FR3: Utilize resources effectively to support continuous operation.

revise.why: To explicitly define interfaces and reduce coupling/diffusion risks between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and NGHE Solution modules.

assumptions: Vehicle control algorithms can be modularized and assigned to Vehicle module.
Interfaces with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules can be clearly defined.
open_risks: Potential diffusion of control logic across modules.
Interface complexity leading to integration challenges.
evidence_needed: Interface specifications between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and NGHE Solution modules.
Preliminary integration test results demonstrating modular interaction.
